title: Project registry service with file system persistence
purpose: Manages a persistent registry of project paths with metadata including access timestamps, supporting operations to register, list, unregister, and touch projects.
module_context: Exports a ProjectRegistry class and a singleton instance for managing project metadata in a JSON file stored in the user's home directory.

steps.content: Check if the registry file exists at the configured path.
IF the file does not exist, return an empty projects array.
Read the file contents as UTF-8 text.
Parse the JSON content into an object.
IF the parsed data does not contain a valid projects array, log a warning and return an empty projects array.
Return the parsed projects data.
Create the parent directory for the registry file if it does not exist.
Write the registry data to the file as formatted JSON with two-space indentation.
Validate that the provided path is absolute.
IF the path is not absolute, throw an error.
Validate that the path exists on the filesystem.
IF the path does not exist, throw an error.
Load the existing registry data.
Generate a current timestamp for the last access time.
Check if a project with the same path already exists in the registry.
IF the project exists, update its last access time and save the registry.
IF the project does not exist, add it to the registry with a name and last access time, then save the registry.
Load the existing registry data.
Initialize arrays to hold valid and stale projects.
For each project in the registry, check if its path exists on the filesystem.
IF the path exists, add the project to the valid projects list.
IF the path does not exist, add the project to the stale projects list.
IF stale projects were found, remove them from the registry and save the updated registry.
Sort the valid projects by last access time in descending order.
Return the sorted list of valid projects.
Load the existing registry data.
Store the initial number of projects in the registry.
Filter out any project that matches the provided path.
IF the number of projects decreased after filtering, save the updated registry.
Return true if a project was removed, false otherwise.
Load the existing registry data.
Find the project in the registry that matches the provided path.
IF the project is found, update its last access time to the current timestamp.
Save the updated registry.
